text: Father Michael, whose practiced hands moved with a slow and steady grace that spoke of long service to men’s souls, poured the wine, a rite he had performed far more times than he could possibly count, whilst the young man who now knelt before him, whose downcast eyes and trembling hands clearly betrayed a mighty inner war, bore a load that felt of a singular, crushing weight. This lad recounted a moment of sudden rage, a raw rush of beastly impulse that had propelled him to a deed he now struggled to comprehend, and the profound sorrow that emanated from him seemed a choking fog, clinging to him as if it were a very cloak. I observed the boy’s knuckles, pale as bone against the dark wood of the pew which he clutched with a desperate grip, and Father Michael, a man who understood that the arduous road to making amends was seldom swift or straightforward to traverse—a journey that commenced with the sheer grit required to speak the truth—recognized the necessity of revealing the deepest shadows of his heart and earnestly seeking God’s pardon, however distant and faint it appeared.

This lad recounted a moment of sudden rage, a raw rush of beastly impulse that had propelled him to a deed he now struggled to comprehend, and the profound sorrow that had emanated from him seemed a choking fog, clinging to him as if it had been a very cloak. I observed the boy’s knuckles, pale as bone against the dark wood of the pew which he clutched with a desperate grip, and Father Michael, a man who understood that the arduous road to making amends had seldom been swift or straightforward to traverse—a journey that had commenced with the sheer grit required to speak the truth—recognized the necessity of revealing the deepest shadows of his heart and earnestly seeking God’s pardon, however distant and faint it had appeared. I felt the solemnity of the moment settle over the chapel, a palpable stillness that underscored the gravity of the young man's confession.

This lad recounted a moment of sudden rage, a raw rush of beastly impulse that had borne him to a deed he struggled to grasp, and the profound sorrow that rose from him seemed a choking mist, clinging to him as if it were a very garment. I marked the boy’s knuckles, pale as bone against the dark wood of the pew which he gripped with a desperate hold, and Father Michael, a man who knew that the arduous path to making amends was seldom swift or simple to tread—a journey that had begun with the sheer stoutness needed to speak the truth—saw the need to lay bare the deepest shades of his heart and earnestly seek God’s forgiveness, however far and dim it seemed. I felt the still heaviness of the hour settle upon us, a common knowing of the strife.
